coredata.dc:title: Biosynthesized Fe-C-dots nanozymes modulate growth, physiological and phytochemical peculiarity to improve saline-alkaline stress tolerance in wheat

coredata.dc:description: Nanotechnology has shown great potential to improve agricultural production and increase crop tolerance to abiotic stresses, including saline-alkaline environments. This study focuses on the biological mechanism of biocompatible iron-doped carbon dots (Fe-C-dots) nanozyme biosynthesized from artemisinin extract to alleviate saline-alkaline stress in wheat (Triticum aestivum L.). Particularly, Fe-C-dots with two types of natural enzyme mimicking properties, target reactive oxygen species (ROS) to assuage oxidative damage and to enhance the antioxidant capacity of enzyme-activated systems. Exogenous application of Fe-C-dots (50 mg/L) significantly promoted wheat growth and increased photosynthetic pigment content and photosynthetic efficiency. At the molecular level, Fe-C-dots treatment activated the nitrogen metabolism pathway of roots, up-regulated the expression of related genes OsNRT2.1, OsGS1, and NADH-GOGAT, and promoted the accumulation of nitrogen in wheat. Transcriptomics and metabolomics analyses reveal that Fe-C-dots triggered metabolic and transcriptional reprogramming in wheat seedlings. Besides, Fe-C-dots activated stress signaling and defense-related pathways, such as plant hormone signal transduction, MAPK, and photosynthesis signaling pathways, the cutin, suberin, wax, flavonoids, and phenolic acids biosynthesis. Importantly, compared to the control group, the application of Fe-C-dots under saline-alkaline stress increased the net photosynthetic rate (Pn) and transpiration rate (Tr) in wheat shoots by 77.5 %, and 78.6 %, respectively. These findings suggested that Fe-C-dots can improve root nitrogen metabolism and stem photosynthesis of wheat, as well as the synthesis of related stress-resistant compounds to cope with the damage of saline-alkaline stress on wheat growth. However, further studies are needed to explore the effectiveness of Fe-C-dots in wheat yield and quality evaluation.

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This study was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Grant No. 3237152, and 22104102), the Natural Science Foundation of Sichuan Province (Grant No. 2025ZNSFSC0155), the Open Project Program (Grant No. SKL-KF202416) of State Key Laboratory of Crop Gene Exploration and Utilization in Southwest China and Two-Way Support Programs of Sichuan Agricultural University (Grant No. P202105).

Subsequently, the relevant indicators of the antioxidant system in shoots were measured, such as malondialdehyde acid (MDA), glutathione (GSH), Ascorbic acid (ASA), O2.-, catalase (CAT), ascorbate peroxidase (APX), peroxidase (POD), superoxide dismutase (SOD). The content of MDA can reflect the degree of adversity suffered by wheat (Chen et al., 2024). O2.- is a ROS that leads to cellular oxidative stress and abnormalities in plant growth and development (Jie et al., 2022). This finding exhibited that the contents of MDA and O2.- were significantly decreased by 27.3 % and 25.5 % by the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group compared with the SA treatment group (Fig. 4E and H). Since GSH is the most critical metabolite in plant antioxidant defense system, and ASA can scavenge intracellular oxides and O2.- in plants (Barwal et al., 2023; Fotopoulos and Kanellis, 2013; T\u00F3th et al., 2013), the elevated levels of ASA (40.5 %, SA vs SA_Fe-C-dots) and the reduced levels of GSH (33.5 %, SA vs SA_Fe-C-dots) may be explained in terms of increased stress tolerance (Fig. 4F and G). POD and APX is an essential antioxidant enzyme in plants that breaks down H2O2 into H2O. Due to the antioxidant capacity of APX and POD, high levels of them, can directly scavenge the accumulation of hydrogen peroxide due to saline-alkaline stress. Compared with the saline-alkaline stress group, the content of APX and POD were increased 5-fold and 56.9 %, respectively. SOD, an enzyme crucial in regulating cellular oxidative stress, lipid metabolism, and preventing lipid peroxidation, catalyzes superoxide anion (O2.-) into H2O2. CAT is involved in H2O2 metabolism, accelerating the decomposition of H2O2 into H2O and O2, thereby reducing oxidative damage., which plays a key role in preventing cellular oxidative stress. CAT and SOD decreased by 42 % and 4.63 %, respectively, in the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group, further supporting that Fe-C-dots can respond to stress by activating the plant antioxidant system (Fig. 4I\u2013L). In response to saline-alkaline stress, plants will synthesize osmotic regulators to alleviate their survival pressure. Proline in its free state is widely distributed in plants and is the main organic osmotic regulator. It also plays an important role in stabilizing the structure of biological macromolecules, relieving ammonia toxicity, and regulating cellular redox potential as an energy reservoir (Li et al., 2022a). We measured the proline content of shoots in different groups (Fig. S9), The results showed that under the condition of saline-alkaline stress, the activation of the plant's defense system led to a large increase in the content of proline compared with the CK group, and the content of proline decreased after the application of Fe-C-dots, which confirmed that Fe-C-dots could alleviate the large production of proline in wheat caused by saline-alkaline stress in response to stress. It was confirmed that Fe-C-dots could not only alleviate stress by regulating the antioxidant system but also play a regulatory role from multiple angles by changing the content of substances related to saline-alkaline stress penetration.This study was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Grant Nos. 3237152, and 22104102), the Natural Science Foundation of Sichuan Province (Grant No. 2025ZNSFSC0155), the Open Project Program (Grant No. SKL-KF202416) of State Key Laboratory of Crop Gene Exploration and Utilization in Southwest China and Two-Way Support Programs of Sichuan Agricultural University (Grant No. P202105).

(D) UV–vis absorption spectrum and fluorescence spectrum of Fe-C-dots. (The illustration shows Fe-C-dots under natural light (left) and ultraviolet light (right). (E) XRD characterization of Fe-C-dots. (F) FT-IR spectra of Fe-C-dots. (G) Raman spectra of Fe-C-dots. (H) XPS full spectrum of Fe-C-dots. (I–L) XPS spectra of C 1s, N 1s, O 1s, and Fe 2p.

Biological safety of Fe-C-dots. (A–C) Toxicity of different concentrations of Fe-C-dots on three cell types. (D–E) Toxicity of different concentrations of Fe-C-dots to earthworms. (F–H) Acute toxicity assays of tobacco (F), soybeans (G), and wheat (H). The standard deviation of the mean of each group is represented by the error bars. Significant differences between groups are indicated by diverse letters (p < 0.05, n = 3).

Fe-C-dots effect on wheat growth under greenhouse conditions. (A) Phenotypic map of wheat under different group treatments, (B) Shoots length of wheat. (C–D) Fresh/dry weight of wheat shoots. (E) Roots length of wheat. (F–G) Fresh/dry weight of wheat roots. (H) Wheat biomass accumulation under different group treatments. (I–L) Photosynthetic indexes (Pn, Ci, Gs, Tr) under different groups of treatments. The error bars illustrate the standard deviation of the mean for each group. Notable differences between groups are denoted by distinct letters (p < 0.05, n = 3).

Fe-C-dots induced antioxidant defense system responses and live-dead cells and ROS content effects in wheat. (A–D) Fluorescence staining photos and fluorescence intensity quantification results of probe PI (A, B) and DCFH-DA (C, D). (E–H) Content of antioxidant-related substances (MDA, GSH, ASA, and O2 .-). (I–L) Antioxidant enzyme activity in wheat (CAT, APX, SOD, and POD). The error bars illustrate the standard deviation of the mean for each group. Notable differences between groups are denoted by distinct letters (p < 0.05, n = 3).

Transcriptional analysis of wheat. (A) Venn diagram of roots and shoots. (B) principal component analysis of roots and shoots. (C) Volcano map and KEGG metabolic pathway of roots. (D) Volcano map and KEGG metabolic pathway of shoot. (E) Specific genetic changes in nitrogen metabolism and glutathione metabolic pathways of roots. (F) Specific genetic changes in Cutin, suberine, and wax biosynthesis pathways of shoots.

Metabolomic analysis of wheat in shoots. (A) Venn diagram of metabolomic. (B) principal component analysis of metabolomic. (C) Dynamic segmentation of metabolite content differences. (D) Differential abundance score plot. (E) Heat map of differential flavonoid metabolites. (F) Heat map of differential Phenolic acids metabolites. (G) Differential Metabolite Correlation Network Diagram. (H) Differential metabolites and chord diagrams.
Biological mechanism of Fe-C-dots nanozymes regulating saline-alkaline stress tolerance in wheat.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Nanotechnology has shown great potential to improve agricultural production and increase crop tolerance to abiotic stresses, including saline-alkaline environments. This study focuses on the biological mechanism of biocompatible iron-doped carbon dots (Fe-C-dots) nanozyme biosynthesized from artemisinin extract to alleviate saline-alkaline stress in wheat (Triticum aestivum L.). Particularly, Fe-C-dots with two types of natural enzyme mimicking properties, target reactive oxygen species (ROS) to assuage oxidative damage and to enhance the antioxidant capacity of enzyme-activated systems. Exogenous application of Fe-C-dots (50 mg/L) significantly promoted wheat growth and increased photosynthetic pigment content and photosynthetic efficiency. At the molecular level, Fe-C-dots treatment activated the nitrogen metabolism pathway of roots, up-regulated the expression of related genes OsNRT2.1, OsGS1, and NADH-GOGAT, and promoted the accumulation of nitrogen in wheat. Transcriptomics and metabolomics analyses reveal that Fe-C-dots triggered metabolic and transcriptional reprogramming in wheat seedlings. Besides, Fe-C-dots activated stress signaling and defense-related pathways, such as plant hormone signal transduction, MAPK, and photosynthesis signaling pathways, the cutin, suberin, wax, flavonoids, and phenolic acids biosynthesis. Importantly, compared to the control group, the application of Fe-C-dots under saline-alkaline stress increased the net photosynthetic rate (Pn) and transpiration rate (Tr) in wheat shoots by 77.5 %, and 78.6 %, respectively. These findings suggested that Fe-C-dots can improve root nitrogen metabolism and stem photosynthesis of wheat, as well as the synthesis of related stress-resistant compounds to cope with the damage of saline-alkaline stress on wheat growth. However, further studies are needed to explore the effectiveness of Fe-C-dots in wheat yield and quality evaluation.

The biosynthesized Fe-C-dots nanozymes regulates ROS signaling engineering coupled nitrogen metabolism, epidermal wax layer and gene reprogramming assisted photosynthesis to enhance wheat saline-alkaline stress tolerance.
Image 1

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Soil salinization has become a major agricultural problem worldwide due to extreme environments and unreasonable land development and utilization (Jaiswal et al., 2021). Salinization induces ion toxicity in plants by inducing osmotic stress, resulting in a dramatic reduction in plants. According to the research report, there are currently 100 countries in the world with a total saline-alkaline land area of about 1.125 billion hectares (Liu and Wang, 2021). The increasing trend of saline-alkaline will pose a major challenge to the food needs of the 9.3 billion projected to live globally in 2050 (Fageria et al., 2011). To overcome the above problems, countries around the world mainly adopt measures such as washing and desalination (Su et al., 2023), organic fertilizers and polymer amendments (Luo et al., 2015), cultivation of salt-tolerant crops and phytoremediation (Liu et al., 2020), etc. However, the above measures still have some problems such as insufficient after-effects, the lack of environmental friendliness, and the generation of tolerance, meanwhile, the excavation of tolerance genes consumes a huge amount of time and economic costs. Therefore, seeking strategies to improve the stress resistance of wheat seedlings is crucial to maintaining crop yield.

In recent years, nanobiotechnology (seeds and seedlings with nanomaterials) has emerged as an emerging technology for improving plant stress resistance (Taylor et al., 2013; Wang et al., 2022b). Among the nanomaterials, nanozymes are defined as nanomaterials with enzyme-mimicking activity. Compared to natural enzymes, nanozymes tend to be able to maintain catalytic activity under high temperatures, and acidic or alkaline environments and are inexpensive, can be prepared in large quantities, and are easy to transport (Liu et al., 2021). Such as ZnO (Alabdallah and Alzahrani, 2020), Fe3O4 (Dong et al., 2022), and calcium hexacyanoferrate (Shen et al., 2024), which can scavenge reactive oxygen species (ROS), enhance photosynthetic function, and regulate enzyme activity. It was confirmed that nanozymes can indeed promote plant growth and development, and improve the ability of plants to alleviate external stress. Carbon dots (C-dots) are a kind of nanomaterial with good biocompatibility and high photostability, this phenomenon has garnered significant interest in recent years owing to its enzyme-like mimetic properties. Researchers used C-dots with different quantum yields to absorb light in the UV region and convert it to red and blue light, increasing the rate of carbon assimilation in plants (Li et al., 2021). Another study doped up conversion materials into C-dots to achieve conversion from the infrared to red light region, improving the utilization of solar energy in crop canopies, and enhancing plant photosynthesis (Xu et al., 2022). Wang et al. (2022a) confirmed that under drought stress, foliar application of carbon dots reduced maize (Zea mays L.) ROS accumulation and the enhancement of water absorption were facilitated through the elevation of abscisic acid and proline levels within the root system of maize, showing multiple positive effects. Cao et al. (2024) demonstrated that C-dots pairing was sufficient for the remediation of Cd-Pb co-contaminated soil, stimulating plant kinetic effect and activating plant detoxification pathways. Iron (Fe) is one of the essential trace elements in plants, it participates in photosynthesis and respiration and acts as an active nitrogen-fixing enzymes (Cao et al., 2022). When plants are deficient in iron, chlorophyll synthesis is slowed down, and a significant decline in yield and quality (Liang, 2022). On the other hand, according to statistics, 40 % of the world's arable land is potentially iron-deficient due to poor iron availability and utilization. As one of the sources of Fe intake, iron deficiency in plants affects the normal acquisition of iron by human beings, which can lead to the development of diseases such as iron deficiency anemia. As one of the main sources of global food, wheat is the second-largest cultivated and most productive cereal in the world, with an annual production of 713 million tons. Due to soil salinization, its grain output is estimated to decrease by more than 60 %, which will also adversely affect the quality of wheat and undoubtedly cause huge losses to the human food industry (Li et al., 2022b). Therefore, Fe-doped carbon dots (Fe-C-dots) nanozymes have great potential as a broad-spectrum antioxidant for scavenging free radicals. However, Fe-C-dots nanozymes have hardly been reported to improve crop stress resistance.

At present, the understanding of the interaction between Fe-C-dots and wheat is still in the early stages. An in-depth understanding of the biological effects of Fe-C-dots on wheat is a prerequisite for their future agricultural applications. Artemisia annua extract is an annual herb extract of Artemisia annua dried whole herb, which mainly contains flavonoids, coumarins, terpenoids, phenylpropionic acid, volatile oils, and other other artemisinin-like species (Acquaviva et al., 2023). The use of natural Chinese medicine extracts as an ideal carbon source for Fe-C-dots synthesis can weaken or eliminate the toxicity of metal nanomaterials, thus achieving the effect of attenuation and effective utilization (Pirsaheb et al., 2024). Moreover, revealing its mechanism of enhancing plant resistance demonstrates the possibility of further promoting the use of nanomaterials in agriculture.

Based on the above research, we designed and fabricated the biocompatible iron-doped carbon dots (Fe-C-dots) nanozyme using artemisinin extract as raw material through biosynthesis. Fe-C-dots can not only provide the necessary iron element for plants, but also have the multi-enzyme catalytic ability of C-dots, and have good biological compatibility for the improvement of stress resistance and quality of food crops. We hypothesized that the application of Fe-C-dots could regulate the physiological performance of wheat under saline-alkaline stress to improve the growth impairment, and further illustrate the significance of Fe-C-dots application by affecting the expression of related genes and metabolites. The formation mechanism, structure, and composition of Fe-C-dots were studied. Then, using wheat seedlings as a model, we systematically studied the ability of Fe-C-dots to effectively remove excess ROS, improve photosynthesis, protect plants from alleviating oxidative damage caused by salt-alkali stress treatment, and promote plant growth under salt-alkali stress. In addition, transcriptome analysis by RNA-seq showed that Fe-C-dots activated the expression of genes involved in signal transduction, defense, and photosynthesis. As shown in Scheme 1 , Fe-C-dots have two types of natural enzyme mimicking activities, including superoxide dismutase (SOD), and peroxidase (POD), which can effectively eliminate excess ROS like (O2 •-) and hydroxyl radicals (•OH) in wheat and provide antioxidant protection to mitigate oxidative damage. The good biocompatibility of Fe-C-dots and the effective roots and shoots internalization of 50 mg/L in wheat highlight its plant application potential in alleviating saline-alkaline oxidative stress. This strategy expands the field of application of nanozymes in agriculture and opens new avenues for understanding the biological mechanisms that remove reactive oxygen species and promote crop growth under adverse conditions.

A total of 0.404 g of Fe(NO3)3·9H2O and 1.5 g of artemisia extract were combined with 60 mL of deionized water and stirred until a homogeneous solution was achieved. This solution was subsequently transferred to a hydrothermal reactor, where it was maintained at a temperature of 200 °C for 12 h. Following this process, the resulting product was freeze-dried to yield Fe-C-dots powder. The crystal structure of Fe-C-dots was characterized by an X-ray diffractometer (XRD, DX-2700, Dandong). Testing of UV–vis absorption spectra with UV–vis spectrophotometer (Beijing, China). The hydrodynamic diameter and zeta-potential of Fe-C-dots were analyzed using the nanoparticle size and zeta-potential analyzer (DLS, Malvern Zetasizer Nano ZS90, Britain). Transmission electron microscopy (TEM, JSM4800F, JEOL, Japan) was used to characterize the shape and structure of the materials. Subsequently, the lattice spacing of Fe-C-dots was obtained by high-resolution TEM (HRTEM). Furthermore, X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS), utilizing the ESCALAB-250Xi instrument from China, was employed to conduct a detailed analysis of the valence states and species of the elements present.

RAW264.7, IPEC-J2 and HaCaT were selected for cytotoxicity testing. Cells were cultured in 96-well plates with the appropriate medium for a duration of one night. Subsequently, the cells were exposed to five distinct concentrations of Fe-C-dots. (0, 40, 80, 120, and 160 mg/L). After 24 h of co-culture, the cell counting kit (CCK-8) was used to assess cell activity. The earthworms were also treated with the same concentration as mentioned before and the survival rate of earthworms was counted after 7 days. Meanwhile, acute toxicity tests on leaves are performed as follows: 500 μL Fe-C-dots (40, 80, 120, 160 mg/L) and water were injected into tobacco, soybeans, and wheat leaves with needle-free syringes and marked. Results were observed 24 h later (3 biological replicates for each plant).

The test wheat variety is Shumai 2203 bred by the Triticeae Research Institute of Sichuan Agricultural University. The dried seeds were disinfected with 5 % sodium hypochlorite for 20 min, washed with deionized water 3 times, placed in a refrigerator at 4 °C for 24 h, and then transferred to Petri dishes and placed in the greenhouse to continue to germinate. After 7 days of seed germination, seedlings with uniform growth were selected, and the setup of the four different groups: a blank control group (CK group), a saline-alkaline stress group (SA group), SA_Fe-C-dots, SA_C-dots, which was derived from the screening of the pre-experiments. Detailed descriptions of the hydroponic and soil culture experimental conditions can be found in the Supporting information . If there is no specific cultivation condition, it refers to hydroponic conditions. The specific settings were as follows: CK: 1/2 concentration of Hoagland's nutrient solution, SA: CK group equally treated with saline-alkaline solution (the molar mass ratio of NaCl: Na2SO4: Na2CO3: NaHCO3 = 1: 9: 1: 9), each liter of Hogeland nutrients contains 0.66 g NaCl, 1.44 g Na2SO4, 0.12 g Na2CO3, 0.85 g NaHCO3. SA_ Fe-C-dots: SA group equally treated with 50 mg/L of Fe-C-dots; SA_ C-dots: SA group equally treated with 50 mg/L of C-dots. They were transferred to black, light-proof hydroponic tanks, which were changed once every five days. The operating temperature of the greenhouse was 25 °C, the relative humidity was 80 % ± 5 %, and the photoperiod was 14h light/10 h dark.

Wheat harvested after 14 days of treatment was divided into shoots and roots, washed several times with ultrapure water, and then wiped clean with paper, the shoots and root's lengths were measured accurately with a scale, fresh weight of shoots and roots and biomass were weighed using a analytical balance, the plant tissues were then packed into kraft paper bags, which were dried at a temperature of 80 °C until a constant weight was achieved, and then the weights of the dried shoots and roots were measured an analytical balance. Six replicates were set for each sample treatment.

Photosynthetic pigment content was determined by the ethanol extraction method. Photosynthetic parameters were measured using a portable photosynthesis system (CIRAS-3, PP SYSTEMS), and the third or fourth leaf of each seedling was selected to determine various photosynthetic parameters of seedlings over 14 days. The photosynthetically active radiation (PAR) was 1000 μmol m−2 s−1, and CO2 concentration was 534.67 ± 4.43 ppm while measuring photosynthesis in a greenhouse.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Oxidative stress level and antioxidative enzyme activity analysis
ce:section.ce:para: On 14 days, SOD, POD, CAT, and APX enzyme activities, as well as O2 −, ASA, MDA, and GSH contents, were determined in the shoots utilizing the appropriate kits provided by the Nanjing Jiancheng Bioengineering Institute (Nanjing, China).

Fresh wheat root tips (1 cm) were taken on 14 days and rinsed well with deionized water, and then the surface was blotted dry with absorbent paper. The root tip was fixed with a 10 μM DCFH-DA for 20 min and washed with PBS buffer. The root tips were fixed with a 20 μM PI for 20 min and washed with PBS buffer. The dead cells and ROS levels in the root tips of the plants were observed under the fluorescence microscope, after that the fluorescence intensity was quantified using Image J.

For RNAseq analysis, wheat tissue samples were thoroughly washed with deionized water fast frozen in liquid nitrogen, and extracted using ethanol precipitation and CTAB-PBIOZOL. cDNA libraries were sequenced by Metware Biotechnology Ltd. on the Illumina sequencing platform. Raw data were filtered using fastp. The wheat reference genome was downloaded from the indicated website, and indexed using HISAT, and clean reads were compared to the wheat reference genome. Gene comparisons were calculated using feature Count and then FPKM values were computed for each gene, taking into account gene length. Differential expression analyses were conducted between two datasets utilizing the DESeq2 software, with subsequent calibration of P values. The adjusted P values and log2 fold change (log2FC) values were employed as criteria for identifying significant differential expressions. Enrichment analyses were conducted utilizing the hypergeometric test methodology. Specifically, for the KEGG enrichment analyses, the hypergeometric distribution test was applied in relation to biological pathways, while for the GO enrichment analyses, the test was executed in accordance with Gene Ontology terms.

The biological samples were processed by freeze-drying (Scientz-100F) followed by grinding. Weigh an appropriate amount of sample powder and add it to a pre-cooled 70 % methanol aqueous solution at −20 °C. Vortex six times for 30 s each. The supernatant was filtered processed and stored in sample bottles for subsequent UPLC-MS/MS analysis. All samples were analyzed by LC/MS using both positive and negative ions. The positive ion method was as follows: a T3 column (Waters ACQUITY Premier HSS T3 column, 1.8 μm, 2.1 mm × 100 mm) was used for the elution, and 0.1 % formic acid in water and acetonitrile containing 0.1 % of formic acid were used as the mobile phases A and B, respectively, for the gradient elution. The column temperature was 40 °C; the flow rate was 0.4 mL/min; the injection volume was 4 μL; and the other aliquot of the sample was subjected to the negative ion condition, which was the same as the elution gradient of the positive ion mode. Data were collected using the Information Dependent Acquisition (IDA) mode of the Analyst TF 1.7.1 software (Sciex Corporation, Concord, Ontario, Canada).

qRT-PCR analysis of mulberry genes was performed using the QX96M Real-Time PCR Detection System (Jeramy Technology, China). The protocol followed the reagent and equipment instructions and descriptions of sangon biotech Kits: Plant total RNA rapid extraction kit (catalog number: B518631), One step RT-Qpcr kit (catalog number: B639277). The relative expression abundance of mRNA was analyzed according to the 2–ΔΔCT method and actin was used as an internal control. Primers for quantitative RT-PCR are listed in Table S1.

GraphPad Prism software was used for graphing. A one-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) was performed different analyses were conducted using the SPSS 27.0 statistical software to determine significance. All data were measured in three parallel replicates. The mean separation means were assessed using Tukey's t-test, and significant differences were observed at a p < 0.05. All histologically relevant graphs were performed using the Metware Cloud.

High-resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM) images display the uniform distribution of Fe-C-dots with a lattice spacing of 0.214 nm (Fig. 1 A) corresponding to the (100) facet of graphite. Dynamic light scattering (DLS) exhibits that the average hydrodynamic diameter is 2.53 ± 0.27 nm and their zeta potential is +2.91 mV (Fig. 1B–C). Besides, the UV–visible absorption spectrum shows strong absorption peaks at 380 nm, which is ascribed to π→π∗ transitions of C=C in the carbon core. Moreover, Fe-C-dots have a maximum blue emission peak at 614 nm upon excitation at 466 nm (Fig. 1D). The as-prepared Fe-C-dots are an efficient converter of ultraviolet A (UV-A) light to 600–800 nm far-red emission, which can be directly absorbed and utilized by chloroplasts (Zhang et al., 2020), thus promote photosynthesis in plants. The X-ray diffractogram (Fig. 1E) shows that the peak at 2θ = 24° belongs to the (002) crystal face of amorphous carbon. FT-IR analysis provides additional information about the surface functional groups present in the prepared Fe-C-dots. As shown in Fig. 1F, the hydroxyl group (-OH) stretching vibration peak near 3205 cm−1, possibly due to the presence of undamaged phenolics and organic acids in the biomass, the peak near 1592 cm−1 may be attributed to the -NO2 objection stretching vibration, and the peak at 1293 cm−1 may be attributed to the C-O stretching vibration. The peaks at 1027 cm−1 and 543 cm−1 may be attributed to the C-O-C and Fe-O stretching vibration. In the Raman spectrum (Fig. 1G), 1354.65 cm−1 and 1564.97 cm−1 are the two main energy band positions of the material, corresponding to the vibrations induced by the dispersion defects and the vibrations of the sp2-bonded carbon atoms. XPS can be used to analyze the elemental types and the corresponding chemical states. According to the XPS full spectrum (Fig. 1H), the Fe-C-dots mainly contain C, N, O, and Fe elements. In the C 1s spectrum (Fig. 1I), the peaks at 288.6 eV, 286.6 eV, and 284.4 eV may be attributed to C=O, C-O, and C=C, respectively. In the N1s spectrum (Fig. 1J), 401.5 eV and 399.9 eV may be attributed to Graphitic N and Pyrrolic N, respectively. In the O 1s spectrum (Fig. 1K), the peaks at 533.0 eV and 531.7 eV may be attributed to C-OH/O-C-O and C=O. In the Fe 2p spectrum (Fig. 1L), the peak at near 724.9 eV belongs to the Fe 2p3/2. The peak near 717.4 eV belongs to Fe3+, and the peaks at 711.6 eV may be attributed to Fe 2p1/2. It is confirmed that the main element in the material is C, while Fe doping is successful and the Fe-C-dots are hydrophilic as shown by the contact angle (Fig. S1).

Before further exploration, the biosafety and biocompatibility of Fe-C-dots in wheat applications were evaluated through in vivo and in vitro studies. The toxicity of the different concentrations of Fe-C-dots to RAW264.7, HaCaT, and IPEC-J2 cells was tested. These findings showed that the cell viability was higher than that of the CK group under the treatment of different concentrations of the Fe-C-dots, confirming that the Fe-C-dots had no toxic effect on the cells (Fig. 2 A–C). Interestingly, the earthworm toxicity test also showed the same non-toxic effect. Compared with the CK group, the survival rate of earthworms in all groups treated with different concentrations of Fe-C-dots was 100 % (Fig. 2D and E). Acute toxicity assays were conducted on detached leaves from tobacco, soybeans, and wheat to assess changes following 24 h of injection with Fe-C-dots. All treated leaves exhibit no signs of chlorosis or hypersensitive reactions, confirming the biosafety of Fe-C-dots for plant use (Fig. 2F–H). Besides, the biosafety of the nanomaterials on wheat was further assessed by the germination vigor index and seed germination rate. These showed that both Fe-C-dots and C-dots at a certain concentration promoted the germination and growth of wheat (Fig. S2).

The optimum Fe-C-dots concentration was screened by the pre-experiment, and finally, 50 mg/L of Fe-C-dots and C-dots were used for the experimental treatment (Fig. S3 and S4). Phenotypically, the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group directly alleviated growth inhibition in shoots and roots of wheat plants related to saline-alkaline stress (Fig. 3 A). In terms of shoots, compared with the SA treatment group, the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group resulted in 41.8 % increase in shoots length, 144.9 % and 93.9 % increase in fresh and dry weights, respectively. However, the effect of the SA_C-dots treatment group was not as effective as that of the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group (Fig. 3B–D). For roots, the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group increased the length of the root by 81.6 % and the fresh and dry weights by 102.7 % and 117.3 %, respectively, compared with the SA treatment group (Fig. 3E–G). The effect of the SA_C-dots treatment group was in line with the results of the SA_ Fe-C-dots treatment group as mentioned before. The data on biomass accumulation (Fig. 3H) showed that the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group increased the biomass accumulation of wheat by 125.8 % compared to the SA treatment group. To further investigate the reasons for the changes in physiological phenotypes, we measured the indicators of photosynthesis. A batch of wheat was cultivated under soil conditions after a hydroponic test. The experimental results (Fig. S5) indicated that Fe-C-dots treatment promoted wheat growth and alleviated saline-alkaline stress. Moreover, the results (Fig. 3I–L and Fig. S6) showed that the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group was able to increase the reduction of net photosynthetic rate (Pn), intercellular CO2 concentration (Ci), and transpiration rate (Tr) due to saline-alkaline stress, while, at the same time, it reduced the elevation of stomatal conductance (Gs) due to saline-alkaline stress, The data showed 77.5 % increase in Pn, 78.6 % increase in Tr, and 47.3 % increase in Ci when SA_Fe-C-dots was compared to SA, while the Gs was reduced by 25.7 %. Besides, the effect of the SA_C-dots treatment group on the photosynthesis-related indicators was slightly less effective than that of the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group. It may be ascribed to Fe-C-dots affecting the strength of photosynthesis through the regulation of absorptive light, thus regulating the response of plants to saline-alkaline stress, and achieving the purpose of stress relief. According to the measurement results of Fe content in shoots (Fig. S7), Fe-C-dots can increase the decrease of Fe absorption due to saline-alkaline stress. Iron is an important nutrient that affects the function of chloroplasts, and studies have shown that 80 % of the iron in leaves is located in chloroplasts, and plays an important role in the formation of electron transport complexes such as PSI/PSII, cytochrome b6f complex, and ferredoxin (Nam et al., 2021). The reason causes of impaired plant growth due to saline-alkaline stress are osmotic stress and ionic toxicity, which we assay Na+ and K+ content of roots and shoots in wheat (Fig. S8), The accumulation of Na+ can damage the membrane system of cells, and K+, as an important inorganic solute, is essential for reducing the osmotic potential of plant cells and maintaining water balance. Plants maintain high K+/Na+ values in tissue cells by restricting Na+ entry into cells and selectively absorbing K+ to ensure normal physiological metabolism (Kopittke, 2012), This may explain why the application of Fe-C-dots could improve the growth impairment of wheat due to saline-alkaline.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fe-C-dots alleviate oxidative stress in wheat
ce:section.ce:para: ROS serve as critical signaling molecules in plants, significantly influencing their responses to both abiotic and biotic stressors. Regulating ROS balance is a promising approach to promote plant resistance, growth, and adaptability to the environment (Zhang et al., 2022). As one of the most important tissues for plants to receive external nutrients, root system cell activity has a great influence on plant growth and development (Zhang et al., 2023). Under saline-alkaline stress, ROS was preferentially enriched in wheat root cells. The number of living-dead cells and ROS levels were measured by Propidium Iodide (PI) and 2′, 7′-dichlorofluorescein-diacetic acid (DCFH-DA) staining, respectively (Fig. 4 A and C). Based on the results of fluorescent probe staining, we found that the average fluorescence intensity of apical dead cell staining in the SA group was reduced by 85.5 % after treatment with Fe-C-dots (Fig. 4B). Thus, it was confirmed that SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group improved cell death due to stress. Then, based on the results of staining root tips for ROS (Fig. 4D), the average fluorescence intensity of root tips was reduced by 62.9 % after the application of Fe-C-dots under saline-alkaline stress conditions. This proved that Fe-C-dots could indeed reduce the accumulation of ROS in the root tips due to saline-alkaline stress.

Subsequently, the relevant indicators of the antioxidant system in shoots were measured, such as malondialdehyde acid (MDA), glutathione (GSH), Ascorbic acid (ASA), O2 .-, catalase (CAT), ascorbate peroxidase (APX), peroxidase (POD), superoxide dismutase (SOD). The content of MDA can reflect the degree of adversity suffered by wheat (Chen et al., 2024). O2 .- is a ROS that leads to cellular oxidative stress and abnormalities in plant growth and development (Jie et al., 2022). This finding exhibited that the contents of MDA and O2 .- were significantly decreased by 27.3 % and 25.5 % by the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group compared with the SA treatment group (Fig. 4E and H). Since GSH is the most critical metabolite in plant antioxidant defense system, and ASA can scavenge intracellular oxides and O2 .- in plants (Barwal et al., 2023; Fotopoulos and Kanellis, 2013; Tóth et al., 2013), the elevated levels of ASA (40.5 %, SA vs SA_Fe-C-dots) and the reduced levels of GSH (33.5 %, SA vs SA_Fe-C-dots) may be explained in terms of increased stress tolerance (Fig. 4F and G). POD and APX is an essential antioxidant enzyme in plants that breaks down H2O2 into H2O. Due to the antioxidant capacity of APX and POD, high levels of them, can directly scavenge the accumulation of hydrogen peroxide due to saline-alkaline stress. Compared with the saline-alkaline stress group, the content of APX and POD were increased 5-fold and 56.9 %, respectively. SOD, an enzyme crucial in regulating cellular oxidative stress, lipid metabolism, and preventing lipid peroxidation, catalyzes superoxide anion (O2 .-) into H2O2. CAT is involved in H2O2 metabolism, accelerating the decomposition of H2O2 into H2O and O2, thereby reducing oxidative damage., which plays a key role in preventing cellular oxidative stress. CAT and SOD decreased by 42 % and 4.63 %, respectively, in the SA_Fe-C-dots treatment group, further supporting that Fe-C-dots can respond to stress by activating the plant antioxidant system (Fig. 4I–L). In response to saline-alkaline stress, plants will synthesize osmotic regulators to alleviate their survival pressure. Proline in its free state is widely distributed in plants and is the main organic osmotic regulator. It also plays an important role in stabilizing the structure of biological macromolecules, relieving ammonia toxicity, and regulating cellular redox potential as an energy reservoir (Li et al., 2022a). We measured the proline content of shoots in different groups (Fig. S9), The results showed that under the condition of saline-alkaline stress, the activation of the plant's defense system led to a large increase in the content of proline compared with the CK group, and the content of proline decreased after the application of Fe-C-dots, which confirmed that Fe-C-dots could alleviate the large production of proline in wheat caused by saline-alkaline stress in response to stress. It was confirmed that Fe-C-dots could not only alleviate stress by regulating the antioxidant system but also play a regulatory role from multiple angles by changing the content of substances related to saline-alkaline stress penetration.

In addition to measuring multiple enzyme activities for the plant's defense, the multiple enzyme-mimicking properties of Fe-C-dots were evaluated. As shown in Fig. S10, Fe-C-dots feature multienzyme activities to mimic two natural enzymes, including SOD, POD. This allows them to effectively neutralize a range of reactive oxygen species, including superoxide anions (O2 •-), hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), and hydroxyl radicals (•OH) to reduce wheat stress. Thus, it can be speculated that this may be scribed that Fe-C-dots directly act as a simulated enzyme to remove excess H2O2 in the plant, resulting in a decrease in the SOD enzyme activity required by the bud (Dong et al., 2024a; Zhang et al., 2024).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fe-C-dots induce transcriptional changes in wheat
ce:section.ce:para: Firstly, the differential gene Venn diagram (Fig. 5 A) showed that for the roots, there are 452 shared differential genes, 5610 differential genes unique to CK vs SA, and 252 differential genes unique to SA vs SA_Fe-C-dots. For the shoot, there are 525 shared differential genes, 5538 differential genes unique to CK vs SA, and 946 differential genes unique to SA vs SA_Fe-C-dots. It indicates that the Fe-C-dots can regulate the changes in plant gene expression under saline-alkaline stress. Secondly, through the classification of differential gene transcription factors, the top transcription factors mainly include AP2/ERF, NAC, bHLH, B3, C2H2, MYB, and other transcription factor families, which are closely related to plant growth and development and stress response (Gu et al., 2023). Afterward, the gene expression was subjected to Principal component analysis (PCA) of gene expression was performed, and the results showed that CK, SA, and SA_Fe-C-dots groups all had distribution variability, confirming that there were indeed significant differences in gene expression among the three treatment groups (Fig. 5B, Fig. S11). Further analyses for differential genes in wheat plants showed that there were 384 up-regulated genes and 320 down-regulated genes in the root system. Whereas, the annotation of the differentially expressed gene KEGG mainly focuses on the amino acid metabolism, biosynthesis of other secondary metabolites, global and overview maps, energy metabolism, lipid metabolism et al. (Fig. 5C, Fig. S12). For shoots, there were 317 up-regulated and 1154 down-regulated differential genes. The results of the KEGG pathway annotation indicated that, except for the same metabolic pathways in roots, the predominantly affected pathways were concentrated on carbohydrate metabolism, glycan biosynthesis, and metabolism, metabolism of terpenoids and polyketides, signal transduction et al. (Fig. 5D, Fig. S13).

Moreover, based on the aforementioned general analysis of transcriptional genes, the different ways Fe-C-dots improve wheat's ability to cope with saline-alkaline stress were studied through the analysis of specific metabolic pathways where the transcriptional genes were located. Firstly, taking the roots as a starting point, we found that Fe-C-dots mainly affected nitrogen metabolism and glutathione metabolism of wheat under saline-alkaline stress, thus achieving the purpose of alleviating stress. Nitrogen is an essential nutrient for plant growth and development and is found in the soil mainly as nitrate, which is taken up and utilized by plants through the root system (Wang et al., 2024). Nitrates in the soil are the main source of nitrogen for plants, and plants need to optimize their root structure to maximize the use of nitrogen in the soil (Lamig et al., 2022). As shown in Fig. 5E, Fig. S14, three related genes, OsNRT2.1, OsGS1, and NADH-GOGAT, which are associated with high-affinity nitrate transporter, glutamine synthetase, and glutamate synthetase, exhibit significant up-regulation in the presence of Fe-C-dots. OsNRT2.1 mainly controls nitrate synthesis, resulting in an increase in nitrate synthesis, thereby improving the nitrogen uptake capacity of wheat, and alleviating root growth slowed down by stress. It has been reported that up-regulation of OsNRT2.1 expression in rice can improve nitrogen use efficiency under low nitrogen conditions (Wang et al., 2020b). In addition, overexpression of OsNRT2.1 could regulate the rice root growth and enhance the overall root length, encompassing fine root length, total adventitious root length, and total lateral root length (Naz et al., 2019), which is also consistent with our results. This better explains the variation of root effects in the physiological properties described in this work. Thus, the nitrogen absorption mechanism of wheat can be referred to as the improvement of nitrogen absorption efficiency and seed yield of rice (Chen et al., 2017; Qi et al., 2023). At the same time, OsGS1, a gene related to nitrogen metabolism, regulates the synthesis of glutamine synthetase cytoplasmic isozyme, increasing the synthesis of L-glutamine, which converts L-glutamine and NH4+ to glutamine at the expense of ATP (Mondal et al., 2021). High levels of L-glutamine synthesis can alleviate the stress-related root growth slowdown, thereby improving the nutrient uptake efficiency of wheat roots (Liao et al., 2022). Besides, field experiments verified that the up-regulation of OsGS1 expression could increase the biomass, tillering number, and flag leaf nitrogen content of rice, thereby improving the yield (Chen et al., 2023). However, the increase of L-glutamate synthesis regulated by related genes NADH-GOGAT further improved the saline-alkaline tolerance of wheat, its primary function is related to Glutamate synthase 1 [NADH] and chloroplastic. Some literature has shown that L-glutamic acid can relieve stress by regulating osmotic substances, maintaining ion balance, interacting with bioactive small molecules, regulating antioxidant system and other mechanisms, and confirmed the role of Fe-C-dots in regulating saline-alkaline tolerance of wheat in this paper (Asgher et al., 2022; Fardus et al., 2021; Jiang and Ding, 2023). In addition, it was confirmed that the application of Fe-C-dots could indeed increase the nitrogen uptake in roots from the perspective of nitrogen, nitrate nitrogen, and ammonium nitrogen content, which was consistent with our above description of the genes involved in nitrogen metabolism (Fig. S15). Moreover, the analysis of the glutathione metabolic pathway associated with nitrogen metabolism revealed that glycine, as a precursor substance, it was found that glycine, as a precursor substance, increases glutathione content by upregulating GSTU6, a related gene that acts on glutathione S-transferase. Besides, glutathione is also used as an antioxidant to scavenge excessive ROS production in the roots and improve the ability of wheat to mitigate saline-alkaline stress. These findings confirmed that GSTU6, as an antioxidant gene, can be involved in optimizing wheat root configuration to mitigate drought resistance (Song et al., 2023).

Next, we analyzed the metabolic pathways in wheat shoots in detail, and the results showed that the regulation of the cutin, suberine, and wax biosynthesis pathways may provide a basis for interpreting the alleviation of saline-alkaline stress. The related genes mainly include CYP, CER, and PXG, which is significantly down-regulated within Fe-C-dots (Fig. 5F). From the results, saline-alkaline stress may stimulate wheat's self-defense mechanism to respond to external environmental stresses through the cutin, suberine, and wax biosynthesis pathways. It is widely known that the cutin, suberine, and wax are the first contact surfaces between the plant and the outside world. Cutin, suberine, and wax have the function of restricting the excessive loss of water from the plant's body, protecting it from ultraviolet radiation, and preventing invasion by pests and diseases (Leliaert et al., 2011). Hasanuzzaman et al. found under salt stress, suggesting that a large accumulation of cuticular waxes forms a protective barrier, which in turn regulates cuticular transpiration, optimizes water use efficiency, and consequently improves plant salt tolerance (Hasanuzzaman et al., 2017). Furthermore, the modulation of cytochrome P450 (CYP) expression has been shown to improve tolerance to salt stress. The constitutive expression of the TaCYP81D5 gene has been found to augment salt tolerance in wheat during both the seedling and reproductive stages by facilitating the activity of reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavenging (Wang et al., 2020c). Furthermore, the AtCYP709B3 gene has been identified as exhibiting elevated expression levels in Arabidopsis thaliana, particularly in response to salt stress, which is associated with enhanced salt tolerance (Mao et al., 2013). CER1-related genes are involved in epidermal wax synthesis, which covers the major aerial tissues of land plants and serves as a protective barrier against non-stomatal water loss and various environmental stresses (Sakuradani et al., 2013). CER reduces epidermal water loss, increases alkane content, and stimulates wax-related genes to resist drought stress in apple healing tissues (Gao et al., 2023). These analyses suggested that, unlike previous studies in which stress capacity was improved by enhancing the expression of genes in related pathways, the imposition of the material mitigated the stress damage to wheat, which may have led to the down-regulation of related genes. Moreover, PXG is involved in the reaction process of peroxygenase, and the down-regulation of gene expression after Fe-C-dots treatment may alleviate the disease suffered by wheat by inhibiting the synthesis of peroxygenase and reducing the production of hydrogen peroxide. It is a key factor that influences the production of metabolites in response to wheat bud exposure to saline-alkaline stress by regulating the changes of genes related to the above metabolic pathways. Firstly, the shoots as the first line of defense against the external environment, play a role in physical defense and improve wheat's ability to cope with saline-alkaline stress by regulating transpiration efficiency and controlling water use efficiency, which is confirmed by the shoot phenotype results (Fig. S16). Furthermore, Lhc proteins are encoded by nuclear genes and can bind to pigment molecules, thereby forming pigment-protein complexes that facilitate the absorption and transport of light energy (Zhao et al., 2020). Lhcb proteins, as a class of membrane proteins with extremely high abundance in photosystem II, are widely involved in a variety of stress responses. For instance, research indicated that Pro-Ca treatment markedly reduced the expression levels of Lhcb1, Lhcb2, Lhcb3, Lhcb5, and Lhcb6 in the IR29 variety subjected to salt stress, resulting in an enhancement of photosynthetic efficiency. Effective mitigation of the deleterious effects of salt stress on rice (Deng et al., 2024). Foliar application of silicon and selenium composite solubles regulates Lhcb1, improves photosynthesis, and attenuates growth loss in hybrid rice under cadmium and lead stress (Wang et al., 2020a). Thus, from the perspective of photosynthesis, the down-regulation of the related genes by the regulation of the Lhcb1 protein provides a rationale for the alleviation of saline-alkaline stresses of the Fe-C-dots from the point of view of photosynthesis (Fig. S17).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fe-C-dots induce metabolomic in shoots of wheat
ce:section.ce:para: Similarly, the differential metabolites of the shoots were first analyzed by the Venn diagram (Fig. 6 A). The results showed that there were 316 common differential metabolites, with 319 and 130 exclusive differential metabolites in CK vs SA and SA vs SA_Fe-C-dots, respectively. PCA showed that the distribution of metabolites in SA and SA_Fe-C-dots was significantly different from the CK (Fig. 6, and Fig. S18), confirming that either the saline-alkaline stress treatment or the Fe-C-dots application affected the metabolite composition in the wheat stem. The up-regulated metabolites are mainly concentrated in amino acids and derivatives, benzene and substituted derivatives, GP, lignans, and coumarins (Fig. S19). For examples, scoparone, colfosceril palmitate, phe-phe-val. The down-regulated metabolites are mainly concentrated in terpenoids, organic acids, such as acetylvalerenolic acid, and so on. The top five positions of differential metabolites were organic acids (22.38 %), amino acids and derivatives (16.71 %), benzene and substituted derivatives (12.18 %), flavonoids (7.22 %), nucleotides and derivatives (3.33 %) (Fig. 6C). These substances have a variety of physiological functions in plants, including participating as signal molecules in regulating plant growth and development, transamination, promoting plant root growth, improving photosynthetic efficiency, and response to adversity stress (Dong et al., 2024b; Gu et al., 2023; He et al., 2024). According to the overall changes in metabolic pathways, relevant metabolites mainly focus on flavonoid biosynthesis, photosynthesis, ether lipid metabolism, etc. This was consistent with the results of transcriptome analysis, which reconfirmed the mechanism of shoot resistance to saline-alkaline stress. On the one hand, by increasing the synthesis of metabolites such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, the chemical pathway of wheat shoots resistant to saline-alkaline stress was enhanced, the content of cyanidin 3-glucoside in SA_Fe-C-dots group was 8.2-fold that in SA group, and naringenin content was 3.12-fold that in SA group. Cyanidin 3-glucoside and naringenin have antioxidant properties, and free radicals scavenging effects (Lee and Kim, 2023; Shahmarbiglou and Razavi, 2024). At the same time, the contents of salicin and tachioside in the Fe-C-dots treated were significantly higher than those in the SA group. Tachioside also has good antioxidant properties, and salicin acts as a precursor substance of salicylic acid and has been shown to promote plant growth and reproduction. It also protects plants from biological and abiotic stresses (Yang et al., 2023). Besides, the synthesis of lipid metabolism affected the physical defense part of the wheat transcriptional metabolic pathway, which enhances the resistance to saline-alkaline stress from the physical point of view. Ultimately, the regulation of photosynthesis-related genes and compounds enhanced the production of photosynthesis in buds, mainly by increasing the production of ATP-related compounds, thereby increasing the photosynthetic rate of buds and mitigating the decrease in photosynthetic efficiency caused by saline-alkaline stress (Fig. 6D–F, S20). Besides, analysis of the correlation and correlation network among differential metabolites showed that most metabolites were positively correlated, which also promoted the coordinated regulation of leaf metabolites and enhanced the ability of the leaf to resist saline-alkaline stress (Fig. 6G and H, Fig. S21).

Regulation of ROS homeostasis is one of the effective strategies to improve crop growth and yield under stress. In this work, we report that biocompatibility and multi-enzyme activity of Fe-C-dots nanozyme alleviate saline-alkaline stress in wheat. In vivo and in vitro experiments confirmed that Fe-C-dots can effectively enhance the defense system of enzymatic antioxidants (SOD, CAT, POD, APX) and non-enzymatic antioxidants (MDA, GSH, ASA) against the critical oxidative damage of ROS-mediated lipid peroxidation and photosynthesis inhibition, and regulate the expression of multiple genes, including antioxidant genes and stress resistance genes. Therefore, it can improve the saline-alkaline resistance and promote the growth and development of wheat. Besides, the toxicity of different concentrations of Fe-C-dots nanozyme to earthworms and acute toxicity tests on tobacco, soybean, and wheat demonstrated the good biocompatibility of Fe-C-dots, which is critical for the conversion of Fe-C-dots into viable agricultural preparations. The alteration of root-related genes is mainly through enhancing the antioxidant system of wheat and clearing its excess ROS. At the same time, at the molecular level, the alteration of root-related genes improves the nitrogen metabolism and glutathione metabolism of roots. Both changes in shoots-related genes and metabolites affect the chemical (flavonoid and phenolic acid production) and physical defense (cuticular wax layer synthesis pathway) of the shoots. Unlike the transgenic method, which is influenced by the plant species and stress types, the mechanism of this method is based on multi-enzyme activity characteristics of the materials, which can directly activate the related processes of plants in response to external abiotic stress. The findings of this research indicate the promising role of nanotechnology in enhancing the resilience of crops to abiotic stressors.
